theme: Compress symbol sequences containing hidden repeated motifs at irregular, multi-scale spacing.
title: Multi-Scale Motif Coder
strategies: pure-literal baseline: encode the whole sequence as one literal run, no dictionary (the evaluator's own weak reference)

fixed-window LZ greedy: scan left to right, match only against the last WINDOW symbols, take the longest match that clears a length floor, else extend a literal run -- decent when repeats are close together but blind to any repeat separated by more than WINDOW symbols

diagnose-then-DP: histogram LEFT-MAXIMAL common-run lengths to find the true recurring scales (immune to noise and to nested-substring double counting), greedily keep only dictionary candidates with positive expected net savings, then run a forward shortest-path DP over positions (given that fixed dictionary) to find the globally cheapest literal/reference segmentation -- captures long-range repeats a bounded window can never reach and never locks into a locally-longest match that blocks a cheaper global parse
